description: Analyzes the support ticket content using LLM to determine the appropriate category from the predefined list
task_description:
{"prompt_template": "Classify this support ticket into one of these categories:\n- billing\n- technical\n- account\n- general\n\nTicket:\n{ticket_text}\n\nReturn only the category name.", "input": {"ticket_text": "I was charged twice for my subscription this month. Please refund the duplicate charge."}}

**IMPORTANT: The task provides these input variables:**
- `prompt` (string) - **THE RAW INPUT DATA** that varies per instance. Your activity MUST parse and process this!
- `_task_schema` (dict) - context for task type
- `ticket_text` (str) - context for task type

**CRITICAL:** Your workflow MUST use `prompt` as the input variable. The prompt contains the raw data to process.
Available variables: prompt, _task_schema, ticket_text

**OUTPUT FORMAT (STRUCTURE DESCRIPTION - NO HARDCODED VALUES!):**
Your activity must return data matching this structure:
```json
{
  "type": "string",
  "description": "One of: billing, technical, account, general - lowercase category name"
}
```

**CRITICAL RULES:**
- The format above describes FIELD NAMES and TYPES only
- Extract ACTUAL values from the `prompt` variable at runtime
- Do NOT hardcode any values from examples
- Do NOT add extra fields
- Match field names EXACTLY as specified

inputs.name: ticket_content
inputs.description: The complete support ticket text content that needs to be analyzed and classified into a category

inputs.name: available_categories
inputs.description: List of valid category names that the ticket can be classified into (billing, technical, account, general)

output.description: Returns the most appropriate category classification for the support ticket content as a lowercase string (billing, technical, account, or general)

workflow_spec.name: Support Ticket Classification
workflow_spec.description: Classifies support tickets into predefined categories based on the content of the ticket
